mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase the discount rate for future corporate earnings, directly compressing equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns during rate hikes are not significantly lower than baseline.

mechanism_narrative: A quarterly increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.2 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions and weakening aggregate demand, which reduces corporate profit expectations and drives negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in >60% of triggered instances or unemployment spikes fail to predict negative returns in out-of-sample backtests.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneously high retail sales growth and low federal funds rates amplify consumer spending power and business revenue, creating a positive feedback loop that generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The joint signal produces negative returns in >50% of cases or the interaction term loses statistical significance in multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: Positive month-over-month momentum in the index of industrial production reflects expanding manufacturing capacity utilization and supply-side strength, which translates into positive short-term S&P 500 returns over the next week.
failure_condition: Signal direction reverses during recessionary periods or momentum fails to predict 5-day returns with a t-statistic below 2.0.

mechanism_narrative: Consistent monthly growth in total nonfarm payrolls indicates a strengthening labor market that supports household income and corporate earnings growth, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Payroll growth shows no predictive power for 20-day returns or Rank IC remains below 0.02 across all rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: When year-over-year consumer price inflation exceeds 2.5 percent, markets price in aggressive central bank tightening and margin compression, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Inflation readings above 2.5% are followed by positive returns in >55% of cases or the threshold loses predictive accuracy in out-of-sample tests.

mechanism_narrative: Elevated housing starts coinciding with rising unemployment signal speculative overbuilding and weakening economic fundamentals, which triggers risk-off sentiment and negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The combined signal fails to predict negative returns or shows positive correlation with equity returns in historical backtests.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly real GDP growth exceeding 3 percent reflects robust aggregate demand and corporate revenue expansion, which drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter during expansionary phases.
failure_condition: GDP growth above 3% is followed by negative returns in >50% of instances or the signal loses predictive power during financial stress periods.
